arts.sum: David Seaman has revealed his worry about Arsenal facing Leicester City this weekend in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: 'This is Arsenal we're talking about' - Mikel Arteta slammed over 'small team' tactics
arts.sum: Arsenal claimed a 2-2 draw with Manchester City on Sunday after playing the entire second-half with 10 men and while Mikel Arteta has defended his tactics, Manchester United legend Roy Keane has let rip

arts.ttl: Jamie Carragher uses Newcastle to make pointed Arsenal response after Man City row
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta grumbled that Newcastle hadn't 'played against any team like this', but such was his respect for the Magpies, the Gunners didn't hesitate to slow down the pace of the game when they faced Eddie Howe's squad in the return fixture at St James' Park a few months later and winning 2-0. Despite Palmer noting that ball-in-play time was 'very low' against Newcastle in 2023 at just 51 minutes and 23 seconds and 'very high' against City, Carragher retorted sharply: "That was nothing to do with you."

arts.ttl: Arsenal player's leaked three-word message sums up private feelings on Man City
arts.sum: Guardiola further commented on teams adopting a defensive approach against City: "If they decide to play in that way, it's more than perfect. Guardiola, the mastermind behind City's success, remains unfazed by opponents' tactics, stating: "The opponents can play how they want to, it's up to us to handle them."

arts.ttl: Ian Wright slams Erling Haaland in X-rated rant after 'disrespectful' Arsenal move
arts.sum: Arsenal hero Ian Wright has launched another scathing attack towards Manchester City sensation Erling Haaland after his actions following their equaliser during Sunday afternoon's dramatic 2-2 draw. The Norwegian split opinion with his antics in light of John Stones' last-gasp equaliser as Haaland ran into the net, before then throwing the ball at the back of the head of despairing Arsenal defender, Gabriel.

arts.ttl: Man City get major Premier League legal battle update as Arsenal, Chelsea and Spurs watch on
arts.sum: He wrote: "Manchester City appear to have secured a potentially significant victory in their legal battle with the Premier League after a vote on APT rule amendments was dropped from today’s meeting. Manchester City have reportedly secured a'significant victory' in their legal battle with the Premier League, according to The Times.

arts.ttl: Martin Odegaard shares Arsenal injury update as positive new return timeframe emerges
arts.sum: Odegaard has since missed the Gunners' last four matches - including the North London Derby and head-to-head with Manchester City - and he's expected to be side-lined until after October's international break . Arsenal captain Martin Odegaard says he's "making progress" with his injury setback.

arts.ttl: Graeme Souness brutally tears into Arsenal over 'inferiority complex' after Man City draw
arts.sum: Graeme Souness has hit out at Arsenal in the aftermath of their dramatic 2-2 draw with Manchester City and is no longer backing them to win the Premier League title
